Turning Akagi's strategy against her, huh? You've still got it in you, old timer!

The Guardian Fox's Orders.say: Exercise Area - Turn 3 (Nagato's Action)
...This sea fog throws a wrench in our plans.
Crafting an environment that only benefits carriers such as herself... I'd expect nothing else from that crafty woman.
Oh, I almost forgot, I'm also a carrier, so it won't affect me negatively. Now if only Zuikaku were here, *sigh*...
By the way, Musashi was the one who ordered Zuikaku to pay a visit to Iron Blood before this exercise, right?
*gasp*... Do you think she purposefully did that to split us up?
I think you're overthinking things... This is just a naval exercise, isn't it? Why would Musashi arrange a whole diplomatic move over an elaborate game of Go?
Musashi never struck me as that kind of person, but when Akagi is involved, you never know!
It wouldn't surprise me if some of that vixen's craftiness rubbed off on Musashi... Though, I suppose I shouldn't be throwing baseless accusations around...
Right, it's hard enough to focus on what's going on in front of us. We can't see Musashi's fleet through all the fog...
If Musashi takes advantage of the fog to slip her forces closer to us...
Listen well, everyone. I shall now tell you our countermeasures for Akagi's sea fog.
Haruna will patrol the territory adjacent to Musashi's forces after capturing the base there.
Hiei, Kawakaze, and Harutsuki will retreat to provide backup for Shoukaku while fending off Musashi's fleet.
We must prevent Musashi from taking the Sacred Sakura under the cover of fog.
Shoukaku will solidify her position using our mass-produced ships.
I already know what Akagi seeks to do, but if I were to disclose it, she would change her actions accordingly.
Remain vigilant, but carry out the instructions I just gave you. Take control of the surrounding bases.
When Nagato's voice disappeared, new symbols appeared around Shoukaku.
Wow... This must be the protection of the gods... I mean, of the Guardian Fox!
Since Shoukaku is the core of Nagato's strategy this turn, she's being funneled all our power and resources!
(I feel a tremendous amount of power welling up within me... What exactly IS this exercise area?)
(Does nobody else feel something strange about this? How are we supposed to get any practical training done when you can "will" incredible power into existence here?)
(Well, I'll worry about it later. Maybe the Commander knows a thing or two about what's going on~)

Piercing the Fog.say: Sanctuary Pagoda - Reception Room
You'll grant me another point for taking control of this base, yes?
Even with the interference of the sea fog, you still took advantage of the situation to solidify your defenses while capturing nearby bases. Impressive work.
Only carriers are able to operate effectively under these conditions. I had to maneuver accordingly.
You protected your friends and expanded your influence. Your spirit is commendable.
But your mistake was not capturing all the bases adjacent to my territory. If you wanted to prevent me from reaching the Sacred Sakura, you should have ensured there are no gaps in your visibility.
Under the fog, slipping past a few patrol ships is a trifling matter.
Are your forces not preoccupied with the defenders at the Dragon Palace?
Do you plan to abandon that course of action and make a straight march towards my territory?
Good observation. My fleet will be tied down until the Dragon Palace is taken.
Unless you never planned on taking it in the first place, right?
You were planning to give those nodes to Akagi, but she's not taking you up on your offer.
Speaking of Akagi, I can't understand why she would take the bases closest to your territory first. Aren't the two of you supposed to be working together?
I don't recall ever agreeing to an alliance with Musashi, Mikasa.
The only thing that matters in this game is victory - friends and foes simply change the dynamics of how to achieve that victory.
I can tell that you're maneuvering to try to contain me, but there's no point for me to entertain you.
That's one way to look at it. We could've worked through our differences in another manner, but you chose to deploy this sea fog in order to obfuscate your moves from me.
Does that not prove you do not trust me?
Who knows? I'm sure you of all people already know what I'm trying to accomplish, whether or not you can see my actions.
I will say this once more - I will achieve victory using the means I deem appropriate.
As you can see, Mikasa, Akagi has the resolve to carry the Sakura Empire through rain and storm.
She cares not if she is set back or humiliated, and continues to push forward regardless. That is a spirit worth looking up to.
What is the real reason you're doing this, Musashi?
While victory depends on who captures holds the most points at the end, there is no rule saying that you cannot have your retainers fight directly.
When shipgirls clash with the full extent of their mental fortitude and will-given form, what we actually witness is a contest of courage and determination.
Just as Nagato's desire to protect Shoukaku made her stronger, my fleet has developed their powers through the confidence I instilled within them.
As Suzutsuki and her allies became more familiar with their special techniques, the Tester vessel was taken down in no time, and the Dragon Palace soon fell to Musashi's forces.
Sakawa and Wakatsuki, freed from their previous battle, quickly made their way towards Nagato's territory just as Musashi had ordered.
Ugh... Where did those two come from?
That area is only defended by Harutsuki and Kawakaze... Musashi, are you...
Going to force our friends to fight each other?!
Force? Hardly. This was just a natural consequence of Akagi deploying her sea fog, and you deciding to reveal your commands to the rest of us.
I won't let you have your way!
Try me then. Show me what determination the child-made-guardian is capable of mustering.

Heart of a Guardian.say: Exercise Area - Turn 3 (Musashi's Action)
Taking advantage of the thick sea fog, Musashi's "retainers," Sakawa and Wakatsuki, crossed over to launch a surprise attack on the area occupied by Nagato's fleet.
Harutsuki! Kawakaze! Musashi's forces are headed towards you!
G-got it! But, where are they coming from? I can't really see through all this fog...
They're coming straight at us! Prepare for combat, Harutsuki!
H-huuuh?!
As soon as Kawakaze drew her blade, Sakawa and Wakatsuki burst from the fog.
Hello there, Nagato's retainers~!
I'm the Agano-class light cruiser, Sakawa. Let's have a fair fight, okay?
And I'm the Akizuki-class destroyer, Wakatsuki. Sorry for barging in all of a sudden, Harutsuki.
I'll be fine... Probably...
Harutsuki, remain alert. We're not allies anymore, for the time being.
Well, we don't necessarily have to fight, right? Musashi only ordered for us to move to this area.
Plus, we just finished taking the Dragon Palace. I could use some extra time for rest and recovery~
Phew... I'd like to avoid conflict if possible...
Unlike Harutsuki, who sighed in relief and lowered her weapons, Kawakaze remained vigilant and poised to strike.
It's true that we're in top shape whereas you've sustained some damage from your previous battle.
If you don't want to fight, I certainly won't force you to.
But don't come a single step closer. Those are Lady Nagato's orders.
I see... So you've been ordered to hold us back. Hmm... I know you're strong, but are you strong enough to stop both of us?
Hiei, who was situated in a nearby grid, heard the impending clash through her communication device.
Nagato, you correctly anticipated Musashi's move, but you didn't fully plan for the effects of the sea fog.
If you chose to launch an all-out attack in the fog, you would've been able to inflict more damage. However, that would prevent you from securing enough bases to pin Musashi in.
It must've been a difficult decision, but you wanted to avoid fighting your own friends.
The sea fog will clear soon, and now, it remains to be seen what kind of decision Musashi will make.
The white veil that blanketed the sea began to thin out and disperse, until finally––
The sound of fighting...?! Does that mean...
Musashi, you actually decided to pull the trigger?
Sorry, but we've got our orders. Um, Wakatsuki, you got Musashi's message as well, right?
KABOOM––––!!
Before Sakawa and Wakatsuki finished preparing to attack, Musashi's mass-produced ships emerged from the fog and began to hammer Harutsuki and Kawakaze with a ferocious barrage.
Sorry, but that's life for ya. We're going to have to fight after all.
Didn't you just say the complete opposite thing a moment ago?!
Hey, I would've preferred to walk away as well, but we can't disobey the boss lady.
I was right not to let my guard down...! But considering the damage those two have already sustained, we clearly have the upper hand!
Harutsuki, stay close behind me! We'll settle this with close-range torpedo combat!
Kawakaze drew her sword, her eyes narrowing as she maneuvered to place Sakawa and Wakatsuki between her and Musashi's mass-produced ships.
You've got some keen moves there, as expected of Nagato's personal bodyguard... But I'm no slouch either!
D-don't underestimate me either!
Just when cannons and swords were about to trade blows––

A brilliant beam of light descended onto the battlefield, like a stream of cherry blossoms hitting the water's surface. The rest of the thinning sea fog was instantly pushed aside, and Nagato, flagship of the Reborn Combined Fleet, emerged.
Nagato's barriers surrounded the attackers on both sides, pushing blades apart and rendering shells useless.
Wow, she completely nullified the cannonballs in midair... Thank you so much, Great Guardian Fox!
Mm. You have nothing to worry about with me here.
Lady Nagato?! We were perfectly capable of handling the situation here... There's no need for you to personally enter the battlefield...
This isn't a matter of trust. I've come to prevent a pointless fight.
In this exercise, there is no reason for us to take up arms against one another. Sakawa, Wakatsuki, stand down!
Woooah, busted... Uhh, it's not like we chose to fight because wanted to...
M-Musashi told us to kick back and enjoy the game, so I might've gotten a bit carried away, haha... Just a little.
Please forgive us, Lady Nagato!
There's no need to apologize. This is just an exercise, and you were merely following orders.
As Musashi said, there's no risk of injury in a controlled exercise area.
We spar against each other pretty much on a daily basis as part of our normal training routine.
So, you don't need to apologize, and you should continue this exercise as usual.
We didn't do anything wrong, did we?
If there's no risk involved in us fighting, why did you stop us?
This game of Go is a clash of wills among the players outside. As such, the combat power of the ships inside is limited, while the importance of one's convictions is amplified.

In other words, you won't accomplish anything from fighting each other in the conventional, physical sense. Rather, the only thing that matters is a clash of ideals - something that cannot happen when you are simply "following orders."
And my conviction is this: there is no place for meaningless conflict. That is the path I shall demonstrate to the Sakura Empire, and the ideal I wish to demonstrate to Musashi and Akagi.
Well spoken! I must say, I am quite moved by your speech. The mighty shield you've produced must be a manifestation of your mental image, right?
Most likely. Perhaps due to the time I spent sealed inside the Sacred Sakura, this domain also reflected the vivid memories I have of that place.
But now, I am no longer the same person I was before. This may be the last time we see a scenery like this here.
In any case, there is no more need for us to battle each other. If you remain unconvinced, then you will have to go through me first.
As long as I stand, nobody will lay a finger on any of my friends!

A New Path.say: Sanctuary Pagoda - Reception Room
Nagato, I wasn't expecting you to enter the battle so soon.
To think that a bit of testing the waters would trigger such a strong rebuke... Perhaps I underestimated that girl's resolve.
Maybe not. That might've just been what Nagato wanted the whole time - to stand on the front lines.
She would put herself in harm's way just to prevent unnecessary conflict...
Because of that antiquated ego, she fails to see the bigger picture. Her fixation on "protecting" what is dear to her may well cause her to lose everything. How foolish.
There is no future to be had down that road, with or without fighting.
Regardless, by sacrificing her advantage as a "player" in this match, her forces now vastly eclipse ours in combat effectiveness.
So, she insists on standing in the way... Mikasa, do you have what it takes to keep up with her?
Keep up? Hah, I don't have the energy or determination that girl has. The most I can do is to fill in the gaps behind her as she forges a new path forward.
Now that the balance of power has been broken, shouldn't we go back to securing as many bases as we can instead of fighting each other?
You may be right in saying that Nagato is forging a new path forward.
But does it not fall upon us to determine if that path leads to the light... or to the abyss?

As someone responsible for shouldering the Sakura Empire's future, there are many other things to consider than your own narrow sense of justice. One cannot make judgments based on speculation and assumptions alone.
I cannot deny what you have done for us. But at the same time, it is too dangerous to work with the Sirens.
The Commander informed me about the Iron Blood incident not too long ago. Unlike in our case, the Commander was involved with their plan to a far greater degree.
Plus, both Bismarck and Friedrich shared the same vision despite their diverging paths... That is why they were able to complete their lengthy project in the end.
If Nagato and I could understand the path that you've chosen, perhaps we might've also been able to work together towards a common goal.
But, your heart is just as opaque as the sea fog concealing this board.
...In order to outwit the Sirens, you must first learn the taste of the poison they serve.
Akagi, you...
After the success of the Iron Blood rebellion, the Sirens have become much more vigilant.
Now that Nagato has made her intentions clear, I will crush her and demonstrate the superiority of my vision.
With Akagi's declaration, a dazzling light enveloped both her and the board, just as it had with Nagato previously.
When the light faded, only two people remained around the board.
She also wants to prove herself, huh... Just like Nagato, she too can only do things in her own way.
The balance of power has again been restored with Akagi's entry into the battle.
She will most likely join up with Miyuki to cut you off from Nagato's forces.
Now the question is, will she try to gobble up your bases, or will she launch an attack on the central tower?
She's not here anymore, so it'll be harder to read her intentions.
It would appear that the youngsters are too impatient to keep our little game of chess going. Hahaha!
Hehe. I'm sure you were no less hot-blooded yourself during that war. Isn't that right, Great Veteran?
But that was a completely different era, Musashi. A long, long time ago, wasn't it?
Even if I tried to run off like them, my body wouldn't move. After all, there are people leaning on me now.
...Musashi, be honest with me. This exercise area is actually a Mirror Sea, isn't it?
What makes you say that?
Mapping one's mental imagery into existence, the sudden disappearance of Nagato and Akagi... Those aren't things that should be possible through the Sakura Islands' rituals.
I wonder, do you really know everything there is to know about our rituals?
...Probably not.
But, that's why I want to know. Pardon me if it seemed like I was accusing you.
No, there's no need for that. Your instincts were correct - this is indeed a Mirror Sea, entrusted to Akagi by the Sirens.
Originally, it was a Siren experimental site that none of us could use. But after combining it with spirit barriers, I was able to turn it into the domain you now see.
I'm not sure if what you've done is amazing or borderline heretical...
I'll take that as high praise from the Great Veteran. I do wonder though... Has this place been to your liking?
I don't think it matters whether or not I "like" it. The reality is, I'm stunned by the number of things here that seem to defy conventional wisdom.
And even more impressively, you're able to control these things. Perhaps the stories of the Iron Blood singularity aren't as far-fetched as I initially believed.
I've also heard of a Sculpting Room that generates storms to keep everyone who does not possess the "Key" away.
In any case, the actual Mirror Sea itself is sealed away, but I did not disclose this to you or Nagato knowing the suspicions both of you have towards Siren technology.
There was once a period of time when I was paranoid of Akagi's every move. At least now, I have fewer doubts about her intentions.
By the way, how do you command your allies after entering the battlefield? To my understanding, our communications are not affected the way they would be in a Mirror Sea?
We're able to give instructions directly to the entire fleet from here, and losing that ability would be a huge disadvantage.
Don't worry. You won't be able to immediately grasp everyone's positions at once, but you will still be able to issue commands to them as before.
I see, so there aren't as many disadvantages as I imagined. So, for our allies inside, there's no difference in how they're getting their orders?
Correct.

There is a key difference though: when the retainer is unable to hear the player's voice, she has no choice but to obey the order. But if she is able to voice her opinions, she may be able to influence those orders.
You will also be able to directly observe the condition of your forces, and that may cause you to make different decisions than if you treated them as pieces on a board.
I wonder if Nagato and Akagi were aware of this before they made their decisions.
I'd imagine so. They chose to fight alongside their friends rather than sit out here enjoying tea with us.
Hehe. Can't blame them.
Hahaha. Well then, as I told Akagi, I will remain here a bit longer.
Show me what kind of moves the three of you will make.

Crimson Descent.say: Exercise Area - Turn 4 (Akagi's Action)
A Compiler vessel... What were we doing while Milord was struggling for survival...?
Ugh... I lost another mass-produced ship... At this rate, I'm going to be forced to retire from the game...
As a battleship, I should be the one on the front lines... But I'm supposed to conserve my firepower and energy still...
Calm down, Yamashiro. Akagi has already taken the base.
Huh, really? Since when...
Before Yamashiro could make sense of what she was seeing, Akagi had already crushed the Siren vessel.
Burning petals swirled around her body, the only remaining traces of the Siren that had once been there.
Akagi!
When I saw your foxflames appear, I thought you had used another command. I didn't think you would directly enter the battlefield...
I was going to fall asleep if I had to spend another minute in that cramped room. Just needed a little change in scenery.
Akagi, what did you just do to that Compiler...
Compiler models are known for having extremely high resistance to conventional weapons. However, we are able to use the unique features of this area to our advantage.
I changed the characteristics of my attack, as you can see from these lycoris flowers here, allowing my flames to penetrate the vessel's weak points.
Knowledge is power - the same is true for fighting Sirens as it is for understanding our opposition.
(The sea fog had a fairly significant effect the first time it was used, but Mikasa will know how to deal with it if I use it a second time.)
Nagato has also descended upon the battlefield. If we do not act quickly, there will be no more bases left to take, and we will be forced into a stalemate.
Understood. We trust your judgment, Akagi.
That's fine, but your own safety comes first. The same goes for Jintsuu and the others.
Color me surprised, though... I didn't expect to see another Compiler so soon. Seems like the ghosts of the past are all coming out to play.
I suppose this means that even if the mainframe has been destroyed, its data can still be used to create spares... But what is the point in doing that––
Thank you for saving us, Akagi! If it weren't for you, all our mass-produced ships probably would've gotten wiped out!
...Do you think I'd exhaust our resources securing a base of this size? I sent the mass-produced ships in to gauge the opponent's specs.
Yamashiro, you will be needed to secure a more important objective.
A more important objective...? Are you talking about the tower over there? I'm not sure I'll be able to take down even stronger enemies...
Hold on. I'm not done talking yet.
Mikasa has already formed a perimeter through the southern side of the board. If we allow her forces to join up with Nagato's, we're as good as done.
Jintsuu, I'd like you to join up with Miyuki. Cross over past the central tower and look for an opportunity to split off Mikasa and Nagato.
Understood!
Yamashiro, you have the important task of preventing Mikasa's forces from advancing on the central tower. I need you to hold the waters around that area.
I just knew I was gonna get sent there! Ugh, everyone's going to be ganging up on me...

That area is where most of the action is going to be, but with your durability, I'm sure you'll be fine. If anything, I'm sure Akagi chose to send you specifically because you're the best fit for the task.
Oh, I see... Well, if Akagi has that much confidence in me...
Surprisingly, the two of you seem to work together pretty well.
Akagi, let me go to the central tower as well. In case something happens there, it'll be safer to have two people instead of one.
That leaves me a bit short-handed for my other move, but it's true that having two people grants both of you more flexibility.
We should be able to work with that... I'll allow it. Make sure you cover Yamashiro.
Understood, Akagi.
Now then, Great Veteran Mikasa. It's your move.

Focused Fire.say: Exercise Area - Turn 4 (Mikasa's Action)
Akagi has sent a fleet to hold the waters around the central tower.
Is she trying to prevent us from assisting Lady Nagato?
She just took a base, but is already poised to make her next move...
Doesn't this put us in a tough position though? We haven't even finished capturing the Diadem of Light yet.
Mikasa said she can handle this situation.
We will maintain pressure around the central area to keep Akagi's forces occupied and allow Nagato to take all the remaining small bases along the perimeter.
Akagi is trying to fulfill three objectives at once, using speed and misdirection to throw us off. However, she is unable to muster significant numbers for any one of those objectives.
While surveying a map of the exercise area, Kongou relayed the messages she received directly from Mikasa.
Even if Akagi manages to capture some of the bases around the central tower, she won't have the resources to push further west.
Why is she doing this? Shouldn't Akagi know that our forces are concentrated around the Diadem of Light?
That's most likely the reason why she only sent a small detachment.
If she planned on taking any other bases on the western side, she would have to commit a lot more resources.
That's why I think this might be a feint. Even though we have the numerical advantage, we're prone to getting pincered if we get too focused on surrounding the detachment.
Regardless of what Akagi is specifically aiming to do, she's going to keep her forces in an area where they will be able to come to each others' aid.
In other words, it's advantageous to keep the enemy in one place?
What we can most likely surmise is, Akagi is trying to keep us from attacking the central tower.
I understand what Mikasa is trying to accomplish, so the next step is turning this situation into an advantage.
After all, we're in no hurry to take the central tower. If anything, Akagi would do us a huge favor by scouting out exactly what kind of enemy we're dealing with in that tower.
For the time being, focus on capturing the Diadem of Light.
So we'll be directing all our firepower at the bases and trying to avoid other shipgirls, huh?
Right, I just got a report from the recon planes - we're dealing with an Omitter vessel up ahead.
Omitters tend to be more aggressive than other types of Sirens, but this isn't a fight we can back down from.
Don't worry.
Mikasa informed me that this battle will be pivotal for us. Don't hold anything back, and hit that Omitter vessel with everything you've got.
Naturally, I will be joining you too. How could the first ship of the Kongou-class battleships miss this chance for glory?
I will lead the charge! Capture the Diadem of Light, then turn around as a unit and intercept Akagi's fleet!

Resolve.say: Woah, Akagi and Mikasa both managed to capture a 5-point base already?
Yes... The only one that hasn't been captured at this point is this Sacred Sakura... Unlike me, Mikasa continues to show no signs of hesitation...
It's nothing. We've already managed to force Sakawa and Wakatsuki back.
We can't afford to fall behind either. Let's take the Sacred Sakura this turn.
Understood. For some reason, my combat performance seems to have improved after my sister's feats.
This domain continues to work in mysterious ways. Interesting.
Those two girls might've retreated for now, but isn't there a chance they attack again on Musashi's turn?
If you are concerned about that, I shall monitor the situation myself. Bring mass-produced ships with you to avoid being caught off-guard.
After we take the Sacred Sakura, there is no longer any benefit for Musashi to attack this area.
If she still wishes to test me after that, she will find that I am not lacking in resolve.
Harutsuki, I want you to head towards the central tower to monitor the situation with Akagi and Mikasa. Kawakaze will cover you.
Shoukaku, Hiei, and Haruna - the three of you will move south and capture the bases along the perimeter.
Musashi, I hope you stop whatever you're trying to do... at least while all of us participants are in the dark about the true purpose of this game...
If we hope to achieve victory without fighting, we will need to capture as many bases as possible while we still can.
I must see through her words and determine what's going on inside her mind... That is my purpose here!

An Unacceptable \"Reality\".say: Sanctuary Pagoda - Reception Room
I see you've finally caught a small glimpse into this game's true purpose, Nagato.
It's just as you said, Mikasa - she is prepared to do what it takes in order to secure her "bloodless" victory...
...Even if she knows it is a waste of the immense power bestowed upon her. Did you predict this as well?
Hah, I'm not a mind-reader, nor do I claim to know everything about Nagato. But what I do know is, now that she has chosen a path, it is my duty to follow it through.
If Nagato does decide to fight, I will gladly take up arms by her side. Both you and Akagi were aware of this before the game even began, right?
In my eyes, you seem more concerned with keeping Akagi in check than facilitating Nagato's victory.
Possibly? It sometimes seems like you know me better than I know myself.
Compassion for the weak may be a virtue, but on the battlefield, it may just as well be your undoing.
I can easily understand your loyalties and what you are aiming for... But why are you keeping that child from facing Akagi?
Your actions do not pave the path to victory. Instead of showing me a vision of the future, you still seem to be shackled by your mistrust.
Now, your fate is no longer in your own hands.
Mistrust? No, it is a measured anticipation more than anything else. Everything depends on what Akagi decides to do - and I have accepted this.

Once you've been put on the back foot, you will continue to remain at a disadvantage. Can someone who has given up the initiative keep her eyes on the future when she cannot even keep up with the present?
Initiative is not everything. We will find out soon enough if Akagi has the discipline to stay, or if she ends up going bust in her haste.
Heh. Right you are, but what will you do if she doesn't end up going bust?
I can tell where you'd prefer to hedge your bets. I won't take it personally if we end up standing on opposite sides.
Surely you jest, Great Veteran~
At this point, I still have time on my side. You're not scrambling to make any moves either, are you?
True. Unlike Nagato, you are not so easy to provoke.
But surely this isn't what you're interested in. You've had questions for me this whole time, haven't you?
Too many things have been on my mind. Things that don't make any sense to me.
For example, why did you send Shinano to Sardegna, and Zuikaku to Iron Blood?
Neither of these things were ordered by the higher-ups, and these decisions were made on extremely short notice.
Are you suggesting that I'm deliberately keeping them away from the Sakura Empire?
I can't think of any other explanation.
Shinano is a good representative for our goodwill delegation to Sardegna, and Zuikaku already has some standing with Iron Blood following her visit to their floating fortress.
Do you disagree with my choice of delegates?
Then, what about the Watatsumi? I heard it had been returned to us, but before anyone realized it, it had gone missing again.
None of us get to arbitrarily decide what to do with the Sakura Empire's relics. Where is it?
Due to the attack on the Diadem of Light, the security protocols regarding our sacred relics have been called into question, so I had it moved to a safer location.
...Do you know anything about the Watatsumi's origins? The more I look into it, the more I doubt it's actually an ancient relic passed down from generation to generation.
I get the same impressions of the Sanctuary. I can't help but feel that Sirens are involved somehow.
That's news to me. I don't know any more about these things than you do, unfortunately.
Perhaps, I know even less. After all, Akagi's dealings with the Sirens are her responsibility alone. I merely give her the authorization she needs.
Akagi's dealings with Siren technology wouldn't have been possible without your backing.
If you already know this much, then what need is there to hear it from my mouth?
We cannot allow Siren technology... No, the Sirens themselves, to go unchecked! Do you not have enough love and loyalty for the Sakura Empire to realize that?
I have nothing but love and loyalty. However...
The Sakura Empire owes the power it currently enjoys to Siren technology. Even without the Naval HQ's Reenactments, we have already become addicted to their poison.
News of this should have reached your ears while you were in seclusion.
I do not believe for a second that this momentary peace means the Sirens have retreated. Ever since the Iron Blood's rebellion, they've been far too quiet.
Then, would you say the Sirens are watching us, biding their time?
Akagi has led the Sakura Empire thus far not because of her relationship to Amagi, nor because she's actively trying to obtain Siren technology.
It is because she has chosen the path that you, I, and the entire Sakura Empire believes in.
What you see is an Akagi ensnared by the corrupting influence of the Sirens, just as how the Black Cube ensnared Bismarck.
But without that power, the Sakura Empire would have no future.
No future? You're not making any sense...
Mikasa, you know what Reenactments are, right? As long as they continue, we will all be condemned to annihilation, just like Bismarck was.
We cannot allow that to happen. I would gladly give my life to resist.
Akagi said the exact same thing. You are focusing only on her madness and have lost sight of her true nature.
If Akagi's path is wrong, then it is necessary to see the reflections of her mind and heart which paved that path.
Akagi is not as simple-minded as you, and Zuikaku, seem to believe.
Are you saying... that Akagi also seeks to destroy our Reenactment, just like the Iron Blood did with theirs?
You've seen the Commander's report too, haven't you? The Sirens deployed a weapon that causes the very foundation of our hulls to tremble.
Those scenes they showed us were not illusions. They are the basis of the Sirens' Natural Calculation System... In other words, their "reality."
Shinano has seen the same dream over and over again. From that single "reality," we are destroyed again and again in countless futures.
A black tornado that seeks to devour the ocean. Horrendous beasts that defy the power of the KAN-SEN and crush our destined future... There is no escape from them.
As long as we cannot break the recorded Reenactment that leads to their "reality," we will be nothing more than ragdolls dancing in the Sirens' palms.
All those sacrifices we made to push them back during the last war... Does none of that matter?
What matters is... None of us can accept that reality lying down. Not Akagi, and not the Commander.
The Naval HQ's... No, rather, the Sirens' Reenactments... They may be too numerous to count.
Even if we are able to escape the flames of war, the power of those aberrant beasts will still bear down upon us–
Akagi, Kaga, Yamato, myself... All of the Sakura Empire. All the shipgirls in this world. Everything will be devoured... until even the dreamer herself meets the same fate.
...Do you understand now, Mikasa?
That... is not a future we can accept.
What Akagi seeks to do is to render all Reenactments null and void, so that they are nothing more than leaves on the tree of possibility.
As principled and compassionate as you are, your distrust towards Akagi is understandable.
However, principles mean nothing if they cannot be defended and enforced. If you believe your path to be correct, then you must prove it to Akagi - with your strength.
Unlike Akagi and Nagato, you are not weak. But even then, you could not bear the burden of the entire Sakura Empire atop your shoulders.
Musashi...
This exercise was designed to be a clash of ideals, not rhetoric.
But in the end, isn't it too boring waxing philosophical around a chessboard?
Make your move, Mikasa. There are those who believe in you, and in the victory they believe you will lead them to.
Don't keep them waiting too long, Great Veteran.

The Will to Protect.say: As the vermillion light of the setting sun began to filter through the pagoda's windows, both the state of the game and the situation on the battlefield had reached a tense stalemate.
Through the fifth and sixth turns, the participants continued to jockey for an advantage, but nobody emerged as a clear favorite.

Fatigue began to set in, and this began to reflect across the board. Enemies that were previously easy to deal with appeared larger and more fearsome as they ramped up their attacks on the various shipgirls.
What's going on here, Musashi? We're getting completely pushed back by the game itself...
The enemies may not be particularly strong, but everyone's forces have been deployed in ways that would never be considered in an actual naval engagement.
This has caused disarray to spread. While some have been able to accomplish their objectives...
With the amount of damage that's been piling up, I wouldn't be surprised if we start seeing people retire from the game soon.
Is Jintsuu about to be the first to go? What a pity...
I suppose the one upside is, we didn't inflict this upon each other.
Everyone has their hands full just dealing with the mass-produced enemies.
I do wonder about that. If the balance of power is broken, the game may well be blown wide open.
I assumed that as long as you remained on Akagi's side, the stalement would inevitably continue. But, unexpected things can happen.
Akagi's forces have traveled a long way.
But now, they've already converged upon the central tower.
Surely that isn't your idea of a victory, is it?

The two of you, especially Akagi, put your forces through too much punishment just to reach the central tower. You've also lost a bulk of your mass-produced ships because they weren't able to keep up.
On the other hand, Nagato and I have sustained very little damage and can operate at pretty much full capacity.
Your failure to consider this has left you wide open. Hah hah!
And what have you earned for yourself? A slight land advantage? While we occupy our position, you will not be able to assault the tower...
Especially if Nagato insists on clinging to her idealism.
I am quite interested in seeing what matters most to her: adhering to her own path, or seizing the victory she needs.
To those ends, I have a test for you.
I will turn all my ships around and attack Haguro, who is monitoring my fleet's movements and closest to Nagato's forces.
Sakawa, Suzutsuki, Chitose, and then Chiyoda will attack her in turn, and at this distance, Nagato will not be able to lend aid even if she wants to.
While it pains me to resort to such tactics, this stalemate must be broken.
After Haguro is defeated, Nagato's forces will also be exposed as well.
There was one way she could have stopped this outcome, but she unfortunately wasted it earlier.
No matter how strong your will is, this domain will not respond to the same mental imagery twice.
But if that will is shared by another individual...
...Then they must fight for those ideals. Say no more. I will uphold Nagato's vision.
Excellent. My only regret is, it'll be quite lonely here with nobody left to chat with.
Just to confirm one last time: Is this what you truly want, Great Veteran?
I decided a long time ago that I would stand by her side until the very end. I won't turn back now.
There might be advantages to managing everything from up here, but I have friends who are counting on me to do the right thing.
Nagato would sacrifice herself to protect her friends, and there is nothing Akagi will not sacrifice to protect what she desires.
Now then, Mikasa, will you take up the sword, or the shield?
My answer should already be clear. But what about you?
If you and Nagato can emerge victorious, I shall give you my answer.
Hah. If you're going to say that, you'd better not expect me to go easy on you.
I'll be going then, Musashi. Oh, and if you get too lonely, you're always welcome to join the rest of us. Hahaha!
Hehe. We will see about that.

To Stop a Spear.say: Exercise Area - Turn 6 (Musashi's Action)
No hard feelings, Haguro, but the boss lady wants you outta here. Are you ready?
Ugh... I knew this was gonna turn into a pain in the ass sooner or later...
I've been milling around over here for a while now, so why'd I become so important all of a sudden?
Jintsuu's pretty much done for, right? So we've gotta even things up a bit. You're actually not even Musashi's main target - that would be poor little Harutsuki.
Alright, guess we're doing this... Come at me, then.
But just a word of warning - be careful where you aim your attacks. Otherwise, you might end up taking Miyuki out.
Huh? I thought we were gonna have a fair fight! Why are you dragging Miyuki into this?
So, what's the plan? I'm good at fighting with my back to the wall, so don't blame me if Miyuki gets caught in the crossfire.
Why you...! Ugh, I can't just turn around and leave though! I have orders from Musashi!
Miyuki, don't let Haguro bully you! Let's team up and take her out this turn!
O-okay! I'll do my best!
Tch, guess my bluff didn't work out the way I wanted it to... Well, that sucks.
After her attempt to use the possibility of friendly fire to deter Sakawa failed, Haguro braced herself for a lopsided two-versus-one, when suddenly–
Stop right there!
A voice boomed from the sky, and the three shipgirls about to clash all stopped in their tracks.
Huuh? What's happening to my rigging? It feels a bit wonky...
I was able to move perfectly fine until a moment ago... Is this someone's special command at work?
I, I can't move my rigging either... Um, does it have to do with this ring of light that just appeared around us?
That must be it... Is that you, Mikasa?
Hahaha, you guessed it!
As the three turned towards the source of the voice, Mikasa's figure slowly appeared from the distance.
Sorry if I sound like a broken record, but Nagato doesn't want us fighting, and I don't either.
Phew, that wouldn't have ended well for me... Was that Nagato's doing just now?
No, that was my command. I also share the same vision as her - one in which we no longer fight amongst ourselves.
Does that mean... You're overriding Musashi's orders?
I guess so. Aren't you glad you don't have to fight anymore?
Y-yeah... I was just capturing this base on Akagi's orders...
What's wrong, Sakawa? Are you going to insist on fighting?
Ahaha, no, no! I was just, uh... getting a sense of deja vu, that's all!
I don't have anything against us sparring during a normal exercise, but it makes no sense to fight each other when the central tower is still standing.
Haha, I don't mind, really. As long as this game has exciting twists and turns, I'm perfectly content to sit back and watch things unfold!
I'm glad you understand. However, the same can't be said for the mindless mass-produced ships headed this way.
But, they seem to be firing indiscriminately at everything in their path... Does Musashi not care about the safety of her peers, or...
As Musashi's command dissipated, the mass-produced ships also began to fall silent again. However, Jintsuu, who had already been heavily damaged, sustained enough damage to render her incapacitated.
These were unfortunate circumstances, Jintsuu.
I'm so sorry, Jintsuu... I should've turned back to help you...
Don't worry about it, it's just a simulated exercise anyway. I'm not actually hurt, just... a little numb?
I have to say, the technology here is pretty lifelike. But I'm sure I'll be fine as soon as I leave the exercise area.
You don't necessarily have to leave just because you've been determined to be incapacitated, right?
That's alright. I'd just get in the way of everyone else if I lingered around without a particularly strong motive in mind.
Honestly, Sendai probably would've been better-suited for this exercise than me. I guess I'll see you folks later.

The Path to Victory.say: Exercise Area - Turn 7 (Akagi's Action)
The most recent report from my recons looks good. We've managed to control this area for several turns now, and we have a clear picture as to how the other fleets are deployed.
Miyuki is pretty much surrounded, and we just lost Jintsuu. Mikasa entered the battlefield and is sealing off all shipgirls from fighting around her territory.
Now that the Great Veteran has thrown her hat into the ring, we won't be able to avoid a direct confrontation.
Yamashiro, Ryuuhou, I want the two of you to back off and give Mikasa a clear avenue to attack the central tower.
Phew... It was getting harder and harder to fight off the mass-produced Sirens...
In spite of that, you still have most of your ammo and barely took any damage. Do a quick resupply, and I expect you to come back ready to go.
Y-yes ma'am!
Miyuki, just stay where you are. Give us an update on Nagato and Mikasa's movements later.
G-got it!
Kaga, Ryuuhou, I want you two to launch a large-scale recon effort on the central tower.
We need to find out what Musashi is trying to hide from us within that tower.
Exercise Area - Turn 7 (Mikasa's Action)
Look, those are Sakura Empire recon aircraft... Akagi must be getting ready to attack the central tower! Mikasa, should we make our move as well?
Hmm... Have we finally broken through to Akagi? She seems to be leaving a path for us straight to the central tower.
Does that mean... She's given up on trying to fight us, and is telling us to focus on the central base first?
That is one possibility, but she also might've found something there that requires us to be there.
So that's why she pulled back her forces?
She might be planning to use us as cannon fodder to find out exactly what's in that tower, no?
Knowing Akagi, she wouldn't do anything unless she seeks to gain an advantage from it, but we can't afford to sit around idly either.
Kirishima, Kongou, can I trouble you two to get visuals on the tower itself? We need to know what we're up against.
Haguro, continue to monitor Akagi's and Musashi's movements. Let me know if they show any signs of trying to attack us again.
So, how about you and Kinu? What are you two going to do?
I'm going to meet up with Nagato. Even without the central tower, Nagato can still win if she captures all the bases along the perimeter.
More importantly, I need to tie up a few loose ends before this game is finished.

A Compassionate Heart.say: Exercise Area - Turn 7 (Nagato's Action)
Lady Nagato, why did Mikasa leave that base before clearing out all the enemies? Is this some sort of miscalculation?
No, she most likely did that on purpose.
After defeating the rest of the enemies occupying the base node, Nagato looked into the distance towards Mikasa and opened a communication channel.
Did you leave that base for me on purpose, Mikasa?
That's right.
Th-thank you...
You protected Harutsuki until you yourself joined the battlefield... I, I cannot thank you enough.
Don't worry about it. The tea party up there was making me sleepy, so I needed a change in scenery.
Since we don't get an opportunity to experience a place like this every day, I figured it'd be nice to take advantage of the front-row seats.
Regardless, I must thank you again...
What's wrong? You sound like you're about to cry.
Remember what Musashi said? This domain reflects your mental imagery and your convictions. If you don't keep your will strong, you'll be put on the back foot.
I just need to believe in my ideals... and capture this base without hesitation.
Nagato ended the communication, repeating Mikasa's words in her head again and again, a slight sigh escaping from her lips.
Alright... Kawakaze, Shoukaku, let's move on to the next base. We can't wait for Mikasa to serve everything to us on a silver platter.
Haruna, Hiei, the two of you should make preparations to attack the central base.
Harutsuki, the two of us will go check on Sakawa and Miyuki. If you have any emergency repair supplies left, we'll give those to them.
Understood!
Focus on healing anyone who needs it. We do not make foes amongst ourselves in the Sakura Empire.
Are these the rest of your emergency repair supplies? Thank you, Lady Nagato! I'll be sure to put them to good use!
Harutsuki waved her gohei around and offered an earnest prayer. Miraculously, some of the signs of damage on Miyuki's and Sakawa's riggings seemed to recede.
Woah... What a warm sensation... Th-thank you very much...
I can feel Harutsuki's prayers healing me up! Ahh, even after everything I've done, you're still so kind to me, Lady Nagato!
Sakawa, I know why Musashi sent you over and over. But no matter how many times she tries, she will not shake my resolve.
Look, I really appreciate you giving me the benefit of the doubt... But if Musashi orders it, I'm going to have to attack you again.
We'll cross that bridge when we get there. But nothing changes the fact that you're also a member of the Sakura Empire and one of our friends.
Ahh, you're so pure-hearted it hurts to look at you! Buuut, you'd better be careful not to let others take advantage of that~
Musashi was right about one thing - this place definitely HAS been quite the experience. Too bad my sisters aren't here to witness it as well.
Speaking of your sisters... I haven't seen Yura in a while. Do you know where she is?
Hmm... I heard Musashi gave her some top-secret mission not too long ago, but I obviously don't know the details beyond that...
Why, did you need her for something?
No, I was just curious.
Phew... That concludes my turn.
I can only hope Musashi comes to understand my intentions.

Tower.say: Exercise Area - Turn 7 (Musashi's Action)
When Musashi's turn rolled around, Sakawa, who was still with Nagato's fleet, suddenly found herself surrounded by purple butterflies.
What is she planning to do this time...?
I suppose I'll be heading towards the tower now. These butterflies seem to be patching me up and making me stronger.
Lady Nagato, I think you've gotten your point across! Hopefully we won't have to fight anymore!
I was just wondering why we had to jump through so many hoops in the first place. I thought there'd be a lot more competition over the big 10-point base in the middle!
Sakawa looked towards the imposing tower in the middle of the exercise area. Akagi had tried to scout it out earlier, but waves of Siren enemies made any movement around the area difficult.
One after another, ships from the four fleets began to arrive around the tower.
That ominous red aura was spooky enough from a distance, but up close, the thing looks like it might even be alive!
Ugh, please don't say that. The way the light is flickering makes it seem like it's breathing...
The other four facilities here were some of the Sakura Empire's most sacred sites... So why would this Siren facility be sitting in the middle of this whole place?
Do you remember hearing from Akagi and the Commander that they also had to destroy some kind of tower during the Iron Blood rebellion?
Do you think this tower represents some higher form of Siren?
How much "higher" can it be if it lost the ability to move around? Chitose, let's go smash that thing up, just the two of us!
O-okay...! Um, also, I've decided on a name for our combo attack... F-Featherstorm!
Is everyone showing off the special techniques they've developed here? I guess I have no choice but to join in on the fun myself––
Ahem! ...I am the piercer of the blue waves, inheritor of the Rakshasa's blade! True Demon Slash!
Sakawa and Wakatsuki both unleashed a blade of light from their riggings, the rippling bolts of energy expanding to several times their original size as they hurtled towards the tower.
Chitose and Chiyoda's combination attack also landed a direct hit on the base of the tower, a plume of flame and smoke quickly rising in the aftermath.
But in a heartbeat, the tower released a nigh-intangible shockwave back at the shipgirls.
It absorbed our attacks, just like before... Except this time, the shockwaves seem to be stronger corresponding to the strength of the attacks.
That's not much of a "counterattack" though - it's barely enough to rock the waters. That can't be all there is to this tower.
Wait, there's something different about the tower now. Do you see any differences in the pattern of light coming from it?
You're right, it's not the same as before. Do you think it's reacting to the attacks we just threw at it?
It's too early to say... But if it's like the tower the Commander encountered during that battle, there might be something nasty sleeping inside.
Those weak shockwaves won't even leave a scratch on us!
However, the moment Musashi's fleet stopped attacking, a blinding flash radiated out from the tip of the tower, tearing violently through the entire domain.
Wh-what in the world was that?! The tower's counterattacking for real now?
Damnit, I spoke too soon... Watch out, everyone! Something's totally different about that tower!

When the shipgirls gathered their senses from the previous shockwave, they quickly noticed that Kirishima, Suzutsuki, Chiyoda, and Yamashiro were no longer present. Instead, they had been replaced by Sirens.
Kirishima... got taken out in one blow?!
I guess the first pulse was just a warning shot... This tower seems to throw back all the energy we pump into it, several times over...
Did the Commander also have to fight against an enemy like this, or...
Three of Musashi's retainers were taken out in a single blow... They aren't even present in the domain anymore.
If Musashi designed this place, shouldn't she have been aware of exactly what she's up against? Why did she waste her own troops like this then?
G-Great Veteran, look over there at that Siren...
The Arbiter that appeared in the NA Ocean...
It must be a vessel...
I see. I've heard all about the Commander's exploits in the NA Ocean, but it seems these towers are guarded by Arbiters...
Is this the surprise you prepared for us, Musashi?
I'll admit, I'm impressed. How did you manage to procure data on an Arbiter and even manufacture a vessel for it?
Shoukaku, you remember this Siren too, don't you? We encountered it briefly in the heart of the NA Ocean.
I won't forgive it for what it did... Making such a vile imitation of Amagi...
Hahaha... Hahahahaha... Even if you're nothing more than a vessel, I'll make you pay!
If this is what I was invited here to do, then I will entertain you, Musashi!

A Love for the Weak.say: There's nothing wrong with letting someone else win. But do you know what it means to entrust your will to someone else?
Musashi now sat alone at the board, quietly overlooking the situation in the exercise area.
Unlike Nagato and Akagi, you already have true strength. It is unsurprising that we would not be able to see eye-to-eye.
Just as Musashi furrowed her brow in discontent, a familiar chirping sound came from the window. A small bird gently landed atop Musashi's shoulder.
So you came again. Did I not say that this pagoda would not shelter you from the elements?
But if you insist on being my companion... I can at least keep you safe through the coming storm.
As if buoyed by Musashi's words, the fledgling swallow chirped a few times.
What an interesting one you are... Now, I have a little question for you.
Who do you think will be the winner of this "exercise?"
Musashi gestured to the holographic display in front of her, and the swallow fluttered its wings a few times as if to respond.
Haha, that question may indeed be too difficult for you... just as it is for me.
The communicator next to her beeped three times in warning before falling silent again. Musashi returned her gaze back to the board.
Now that everyone has gathered around the tower... Let us put an end to this game.
I have assembled the pieces for you. I have brought you before the power you seek.
But I can do no more than to set the stage for you. Whether you can become the star of this show... now depends on the strength of your will.
Make the most of this performance, o feeble child.

Retreat and Regroup.say: Exercise Area - Turn 8 (Akagi's Action)
Woaaaah... I could've sworn I was going to sink! My head's still spinning...
Y-Yamashiro... Are you still able to stand...?
What incredible tenacity. Is that the strength of your determination to overcome bad luck?
Ehehe... I'm not sure what happened, but, I kept thinking about how I didn't want to let everyone down! And then, it didn't hurt anymore!
Wait, huh? Are we missing some people? Where did Chiyoda and Suzutsuki go? And is Kirishima gone too?!
That tower almost took all four of you down in a single blow. I always had a feeling you couldn't just march right up and capture it, but I never expected something like this.
Musashi aside, I can see Nagato and Mikasa preparing to attack this tower as well.
Do they think we can still work together? Or are they trying to seize victory away from me at the last moment? Not that it matters in the end.
Akagi, it's your turn again.
After the tower's last attack, the turbulence in these waters has made it harder for certain ships to operate.
I see... Meanwhile, battleships have greater stability under these conditions, giving them a significant advantage.
But we're hardly old-fashioned ships prone to being battered around by the waves. This won't affect how we proceed.
Akagi, should we continue attacking the tower?
No, we need to regroup and investigate the tower more thoroughly. It's clear that it cannot be captured through conventional means.
Everyone, get back into formation first. Miyuki, how's the situation over there?
I, I'm seeing increased activity from mass-produced ships! Should I continue observing them and alert you if they're headed your way?
Very well. You'll have safety in numbers around Mikasa's forces. I'll be in touch with you later, Miyuki.
Understood!
Um... Is there anything I can do?
You don't look like you're in any condition to fight, so you might as well head back and try to capture some of the perimeter bases we skipped over, and see if you can find any emergency repairs.
O-okay, thank you very much! But wow, that attack from the tower was something else!
Ryuuhou, go with Yamashiro and cover her until she can patch herself up.
Yes ma'am! I haven't seen much action for a while, so it'll be a nice change of pace to go back and capture some bases.

The Final Stretch.say: Exercise Area - Turn 8 (Mikasa's Action)
After Kirishima was knocked out by the tower's sudden counterattack, Mikasa's fleet followed Akagi's example and retreated out of range.
I can't believe we lost Kirishima just like that... Haguro, what happened?

Out of the four ships who were too close to the tower, only Yamashiro managed to withstand that shockwave... Damn that Akagi, she left a path to the tower open on purpose knowing exactly what it does!
I wouldn't jump to conclusions so soon. We just have to go back to taking a wait-and-see approach again.
For now, let's also regroup and reorganize. We're getting to the most pivotal part of the match, so stay focused!
Well spoken, Great Veteran! Lead us onward to victory!
This game may well be settled by whoever captures that tower, but it's clear that none of us have the capability to do so just yet.
Akagi has obviously realized this as well. That is why she has also fallen back and is turning around to pick up the bases she left previously.
Mikasa, I don't think I'll be of much use right now due to the weather conditions, but I can continue to perform recon around the tower while taking advantage of my speed and maneuverability.
That's fine. Kongou and I will support you so you won't be threatened by mass-produced Sirens, but make sure not to wander too close or you'll draw the attention of the Arbiter's patrol fleets.
One last thing - we need to keep a close eye on Musashi's every move. Now that Nagato, Akagi, and I have all entered the battle, she is the only one who remains completely inscrutable to us.
If she does decide to enter the battle herself, someone will have to stop her.
Acknowledged.
So, uh, is there anything you need me to do?
Just stay with Kinu and join up with Nagato's fleet. Help her capture as many bases as possible.
Got it. Nagato can win as long as she gets enough of the remaining bases, right?
That's right. The central tower may be worth ten points, but there are ways of making up that difference.
Nagato is currently in the lead with nine points, so we need to make sure there aren't enough points left to overtake her.
In the event that nobody can take the central base at all, Nagato will win by default as long as she can maintain the lead.
Alrighty then. Let's get this show on the road...
Nagato, are these arrangements fine for you?
They're quite beneficial, thank you.
Good. Let's focus on improving our combat capabilities, then come back for that tower later.
Exercise Area - Turn 8 (Nagato's Action)
Everyone, we will be fully cooperating with Mikasa to seize victory from now on.
We will have a small group capture as many bases as possible, while the rest of us prepare to take the central tower.
Once all the bases have been taken, we can throw everything we have at that tower.
As fellow members of the Sakura Empire, we must never turn our weapons upon each other... That is not a compromise I will ever make.
Nagato, even after taking Mikasa's bases, you won't have enough points to win without entering Musashi's territory. Isn't some sort of confrontation with her forces inevitable?
What happens if Musashi actually plans to fight you off from being able to take those bases?
Musashi is not able to win even if she takes the central tower. We will have to hope that she prioritizes helping Akagi over getting in our way.
With Haguro's help, we won't need to commit a great deal of resources to finish capturing the rest of Mikasa's bases.
Shoukaku, Haruna, and Hiei – I want the three of you to start moving towards Musashi's territory.
Kawakaze and I will join up with Mikasa and thin out any enemy forces that emerge from the tower.
If we work together, victory will be ours! Everyone, prepare to move out!

Betrayal.say: Exercise Area - Turn 8 (Musashi's Action)
Looks like everyone's scrambling to snag up the rest of the bases before fighting over the central tower~
On top of that, Nagato and Mikasa seem to be posturing to stonewall us from any attempts to take the tower before they can gather their forces.
I, I can't believe Chiyoda is gone just like that... Are we going to be alright?
I just saw Nagato moving her forces north of us! She's going to try to cross into our territory and capture the bases we left behind!
But even if we defend those bases, I don't think we can win anymore... Do you think Musashi is going to come help us?
I'm sure of it! She's probably just waiting for the perfect moment to make a grand entrance!
Nagato, who descended with the dignity of cherry blossoms dancing in the wind, and Akagi, who rained down like fire from the skies!
Mikasa wasn't as flashy, but she did prevent us from moving for a while... I'm sure Musashi's going to turn the tides of battle with a bang!
Oh, Musashi's orders are coming in... Let's see... Wait, huh? What's the meaning of this?
We're... not being ordered to attack the central tower like everyone else?
I'm... being ordered to defend our bases... Does this mean I'll have to square off against Nagato's forces again?
My orders... don't make any sense at all! Why?!
Um... Miyuki?
Y-yes...?
I'm really sorry about this.
Wait... What are you doing?
Musashi ordered... for me and Chitose to take you out.
Huh...? Why? I thought, I thought Musashi and Akagi were working together... I thought we're on the same side...
I... don't know. I can't ask Musashi what's going on since she's still on the other side...
I won't try to dodge if you fight back, so... Please don't hold it against me when we see each other later! Okay?
I, I'm still one of Akagi's retainers... so I'm still going to do my best...!
KABOOM––!!
Shortly afterwards, in Mikasa's territory...
What?! Musashi launched an attack on Akagi??
Aren't those two supposed to be working together? What the hell is going on?
I don't have a clue either. What do you think, Mikasa?
Sorry to disappoint, but I'm just as confused as the rest of you.
(Are those two on bad terms now? No, that doesn't make any sense... Akagi left the room even before I did...)
(And why wait until now? If this is another way of testing Nagato...?)
(If those two have been working together since the very beginning, there's a possibility this was planned.)
(Now, Miyuki's been removed from the battle, and Sakawa is heavily damaged as well... Think, is there any benefit to purposefully thinning out your own forces?)
(Akagi mentioned something about the Sirens being increasingly vigilant since the Iron Blood rebellion... Does that have something to do with it?)
There has to be something we're overlooking here...
What do you mean? What reason could Musashi possibly have for attacking Miyuki?
I can't exactly put my finger on it, but I have a bad feeling this feeds into Akagi's goal...
If you're suggesting that Akagi WANTED to thin out everyone's ranks from the very beginning, then drawing us in to that earlier "attack" on the tower makes sense now.

I'm not sure that theory holds up, since she could've ignored the bases altogether and attacked us from the very beginning. But it does explain why Musashi barely took any bases and kept poking and prodding at us with Sakawa and Wakatsuki...
Maybe she needs to whittle down the number of participants left to fulfill some condition? In that sense, Nagato's tactics have been inadvertently countering hers this whole time.
Musashi said at the very beginning that this game would be a battle of wills and ideals. By extension, this means...
That she would readily sacrifice her friends to accomplish her goals...
That's one possibility, but she might also be testing Akagi, just like she did with Nagato.
So that's why she's sending Wakatsuki to defend against Nagato alone. It's a win for her if she either gets Nagato to abandon her ideals, or if she can prevent Nagato from taking any more bases.
Damn it... Does that woman believe in anything, or is she just playing devil's advocate?!
That's what we're all trying to find out... All we can do is wait to see what happens next.
(It's too late now, but I fear that none of us should've ever agreed to this "exercise.")
(Had I become aware of the Sanctuary earlier, I might've been able to... No, because of this, Nagato has found her own vision for our future, and I've resolved myself to fight for her ideals.)
(I see... I shouldn't have any doubts at this point. As long as Nagato can win, nothing else matters!)

Ripple Effect.say: As more ships began to drop out of the game from the intensifying battles, the waters continued to become more turbulent as if reflecting the participants' anxiousness.
Everyone waited with bated breaths for the next command to be issued, each move feeling less and less like a game.
So that's what you've been after all along, Musashi... Whittling our numbers down.
Musashi's turn has ended, but I still can't figure out why she'd attack Miyuki like that.
It's all a part of her plan, isn't it?
I'd imagine so. Miyuki was cut off from the rest of us anyway, so Musashi likely saw her as a liability.
So what do we do now? Without her to keep track of Nagato's and Mikasa's forces, we'll have a harder time controlling the central tower.
We'll consolidate our forces and prepare to attack the tower as before. However, I do have a special task for you.
Now that Musashi's tipped her hand, two can play at her game.
Ryuuhou, Kaga, I want the two of you to launch a long-ranged bombing attack on the tower.
Wait, if we do that, it'll trigger another shockwave... Isn't Hatakaze still in range of the tower?
Precisely. If we don't engage in combat with another shipgirl, we can do some damage while bypassing Mikasa and Nagato's pesky defenses.
Why are we still trying to attack our own friends? I thought we were going to work together to knock down that tower!
I never said anything to the contrary. Just focus on taking this time to get patched up. I'll need you again later.
O-okay! I'll stay put over here and let the repairs continue to run then!
That's right, I still need you to soak up damage for me later, so don't get yourself knocked out!
Ahaha... I'm not sure if that's supposed to be a compliment, but I'll do my best!
(Neither Nagato nor Mikasa will let you get your way easily, Musashi... Now, what will you do?)

A Joint Effort.say: Exercise Area - Turn 9 (Mikasa's Action)
Damnit... I can't move my rigging anymore after taking that shockwave head-on... I think I'm done for...
But... this proves that Mikasa's intuition was right. Akagi's trying to thin out our ranks.
We'll have to respond accordingly. We can't let her get away with this.
Hatakaze, retreat while you still can. I don't know how much time we have, but we need to stall as long as possible while there's still no clear route to capturing the tower.
If we can hold out until time runs out, Nagato will be the winner.
Musashi... No, her and Akagi too, are both on a timer. We just need to keep things under control.
Haguro, Kinu, can the two of you take the rest of the mass-produced ships to help Nagato take the remaining bases?
Understood. Safety in numbers, right?
Precisely. The biggest toss-up is what Musashi plans to do, but if we can get Hatakaze patched up this turn, Musashi will have to decide if it's worth for her to finish the job.
Kongou, move north to make sure Musashi can't attack without crossing through the Siren-controlled central area first.
Leave it to me.
(This is my test to the two of you, Musashi and Akagi. You thought Hatakaze would be easy pickings, but let's see what you're willing to do to thin out our numbers.)
Exercise Area - Turn 9 (Nagato's Action)
Lady Nagato, we've finished capturing this point as well! That's one closer to victory!
Thank you for your efforts. That goes for Hiei and Haruna as well.
We were able to impede Wakatsuki long enough to capture the base without having to engage in direct combat.
As expected... Nagato isn't going to take the bait, and I can't get through those battleships...
Once a base has been captured, it cannot be taken away by someone else.
If not for these orders... I would be wishing for your success, Lady Nagato...
...Thank you. That concludes my turn.

True Intentions.say: Lightning flashed, thunder roared. A storm had begun raging outside the pagoda.
Musashi continued to sit alone in the dark room, lit only by the holographic board to which her eyes were fixed.
Until now, there was nothing I could do for you. But now, I shall grant your wish.
Yes, Akagi... Your audience still awaits.
Your tenacity, your willpower. The path of thorns you've chosen to tread that earns you nothing but scorn and distrust.
To unleash that passion bordering on madness, you need to sever all your ties.
For your weak self to finally become strong... I shall give you the final push you need.
An order was given. Rings of light appeared beneath Chitose and her mass-produced ships.
Wave after wave of planes began to pour out from them, the humming of engines filling the sky as the metallic flock descended upon the still-injured Hatakaze.
Musashi knew that Mikasa lacked the ability to ward off a large-scale aerial assault.
Join forces all you'd like. But alliances that fail to produce results are worthless, nothing more than vacuous words on a page.
In order for the Sakura Empire to move forward, our convictions must all be subject to a trial by fire, where every benefit and every flaw is brought before the light.
Are those ideals strong enough to bear the burden of our future, or are they nothing more than self-serving whims?
I may not agree with Akagi's ideals in their entirety, but I believe she does deserve the opportunity to fight for them.
Similarly, Nagato must get the opportunity to show that her will to protect is not pacifism or isolationism, but rather a way of seizing victory.
The question of our future has never been mine to decide, nor is it even a matter of who holds on the most points on this board.
Now show me, my friends. Let your hearts sing, and let your wills be heard.

Darkness Falls.say: Exercise Area - Turn 10 (Akagi's Action)
Losses continued to pile up, and the false sky in the exercise area became dimmer and more ominous.
The air itself seemed to become thick and oppressive, as if signalling some great catastrophe that would put an end to the game sooner rather than later.
It's just as you predicted, Akagi. Musashi prioritized finishing off Hatakaze.
I still don't see any rhyme or reason to Musashi's actions... but she must know something about this domain that we don't.
This originally began as a little board game, but it's starting to feel more and more like a real battle.
Ugh... This atmosphere's giving me the creeps. It's like something terrible is about to happen...

Now that I think about it, Musashi is the only one who's barely been playing by the rules of her own game. Since the beginning, she's been more interested in attacking us. She even used a special command to launch a long-range attack on Hatakaze...
Regardless, we can still take advantage of the chaos created by this situation.
Mikasa might think stalling for time is her best shot at victory, but we shall see about that.
Yamashiro, prepare to move all your forces towards the central tower.
Huh? But aren't we at a disadvantage now without Miyuki? Plus, I'm still not fully healed up yet...
The only frontline we can muster would be... me and the mass-produced ships. Isn't that a bit unreasonable?
Akagi, Yamashiro still hasn't regained her full strength. If your goal is to keep the Arbiter's forces from overrunning us, I can handle that alone.
If it's not a direct engagement, I still have plenty of supplies and endurance to work with. As long as someone can clear the path to the central tower, the rest of us will have a better shot.
...As long as someone does it, huh.
I'll go, Akagi.
Kaga?
I'm more durable than Ryuuhou in case something goes wrong. We ought to minimize the number of risks we take.
...Fine. Go, then.
When Kaga manages to draw the Arbiter's attention, the rest of us will move in.
The endgame begins here. I'll settle this on my next turn.

Last Turn, First Step.say: Exercise Area
It's gone pitch-dark... Man, the weather here is as erratic as you'd expect out of any Mirror Sea.
But it's also an impressive view to behold... almost like a total eclipse.
What a strange place. The weather conditions here all look and feel real.
And if they were generated by our mental imagery, what does that say about how everyone here feels right now?
Now that you mention it, I was so caught up trying to figure out Musashi's motives I completely overlooked that fact.
(Total eclipses have long been symbols of superstition, fear, and great turmoil.)
(Considering Musashi's actions as well as the ominous nature of the tower, it didn't occur to me that we've been caught up by everything that's been happening around us.)
(...How could I forget? Musashi said this exercise area is a modified Mirror Sea, right?)
Everyone, try to calm down. We can't let ourselves get overwhelmed with fear and anxiety.
You're right, things have gotten way too tense over the last few turns.
I suppose we'll have to go see Musashi in person afterwards and thank her for putting on a convincing show. Hahaha!
Now that the visibility is poor, I can't easily tell if there are enemy planes coming at us. Do you think we're safe at this distance?
That is an important thing to consider. However, Chitose's offensive capabilities have been largely curbed with Chiyoda's loss, and Akagi seems to be trying to capture the central tower again.
The fact that Musashi has the last move is proving to be quite troublesome... We still don't have any idea of what she's after.
What do you plan to do next, Mikasa?
I'm still regrouping after the last attack, so let's go over our options.
First of all, I expected to have a bit more time. Now that Akagi has committed all her forces to taking the tower, it's unlikely to see any attacks from her end.
Musashi can no longer overtake Nagato even if she captures the tower, meaning that only Akagi remains in contention.
It makes sense that she would commit all her resources to capturing the tower at all costs.
However, by doing so, Nagato and I are freed up to do whatever we want.
If I can create enough of an impediment to stop Akagi from capturing the tower, Nagato's victory is assured.
As always, what Musashi decides to do will be the most important wildcard.
She still has not entered the battlefield herself even at the eleventh hour, and none of us are able to question her about her motivations anymore.
The fact of the matter is, every ally that we lose makes it harder for anyone to capture the central tower, unless there's another factor at play that I don't know about.
Whatever she's planning, Nagato, we need to make sure we're in a position to reduce the number of moves she can make.
Mm. That would be sensible.
Kinu and Haguro, take the mass-produced ships with you and capture the last free base.
Kawakaze, Shoukaku, keep doing what you were doing before. I want us to be as strong as possible before we move towards the central tower again.
Things never should've gone this way. I would've liked Musashi to lend her forces in assisting a joint attack on the tower.
But, that's no longer possible. We need to deploy our ships in a way that prevents her from interfering in the final battle.
Haruna, Hiei, I want you two to wrap around into Musashi's territory so we can have the most angles of attack possible.
Kirishima was taken out in a single shockwave from that tower. We need to give ourselves as much space as possible to back up.
The three Kongou sisters remaining are still healthy, and if we combine everything we know about that tower into concrete mental imagery...
We might be able to outrange the tower's counterattack range with the full extent of our firepower.
I agree. Kongou and her sisters have had consistently good performance through the shifting conditions here.
Now the last thing we need to account for is, how do we deal with Musashi when she enters the battlefield?
To be honest, I don't think any of us other than Nagato have a chance of landing a clean hit on her. We might have our hands full just trying to evade her attacks.
The one other countermeasure we have is to take out all her retainers before she has the ability to enter the fight.
That would mean Musashi is immediately eliminated.
I know you won't agree with that method, Nagato, but it's the surest way to victory.
Mm. I already said, I will not point my guns at my fellow shipgirls.
A journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step. All the water in the ocean must flow from somewhere...
Nagato, you've held firm to your beliefs this entire time, and we are about to witness the fruit borne by your convictions.
The tower's counterattacks may be troublesome, but we still have enough strength left, as well as mass-produced ships we can use to soak up some of the damage.
Ideally, we can destroy the tower in one turn. But if that isn't possible, we are still poised to strike again the turn after that.
While we control the surrounding waters, Akagi only has a single access point, and her planes will not be as effective as our shelling against land targets.
I will pour everything into my next attack. It's up to you to capture the base itself, Nagato.
Let us show Akagi that the future of the Sakura Empire is one in which all shipgirls work together towards a common goal!

Nine-Tailed Fox.say: Exercise Area
While Mikasa and Nagato were finalizing their plans to capture the central tower...
Musashi still hasn't done anything yet... Has she given up on commanding altogether?
That's impossible – this is Musashi we're talking about here. There's no way she doesn't have her own plans and ambitions.
Wait, Musashi's fleet is starting to move... towards the central tower?
We have orders from Musashi: we'll start attacking the Arbiter vessel at the central tower.
I'll back you up, Chitose!
Did you get the same order, Wakatsuki?
Sure did! But to be honest, I've taken a lot of damage already, so I don't know how much I'll be able to do. How are you holding up?
I lost a lot of planes in the last attack... I'm not sure I'll be able to do much to the Arbiter with what I have left...
I guess this will be our last command, then... There's one last thing I wanted to do here, and I might just get the opportunity to do it!
Mm, yeah... I had a lot of fun too... I'm just glad I'll get to see Chiyoda soon...
Maybe we can grab some sweets once we're back... and see how this game finishes...
If you're looking for sweets, Musashi has a whole big stash of 'em in the lounge! I was thinking, if I got taken out first, I'd go and prepare refreshments for everyone.
After all, sweets are best enjoyed with friends! Oh, I just hope Pooky didn't pilfer them all first!
Then, um... Let's get back to the lounge as quickly as possible!
KABOOM––––!!
And thus, Wakatsuki and Chitose disappeared without a trace.
At the same time...
Sorry, Kaga. Looks like I'll be playing the villain through-and-through today.
